title: Thyroid — implied and overclaim
notes: Synthetic; controlled ground truth. VERIFY before publishing.
manuscript: More patients achieved normal TSH with the therapy than with placebo.1 The therapy significantly reduced goitre size.1

goldClaims.originalText: More patients achieved normal TSH with the therapy than with placebo.1
goldClaims.text: More patients achieved normal TSH with the therapy than with placebo

goldClaims.originalText: The therapy significantly reduced goitre size.1
goldClaims.text: The therapy significantly reduced goitre size

1.name: THYR-1 (Okoro 2023)
1.text: A total of 240 patients with goitre were randomised to the therapy or placebo for 24 weeks. Biochemical and imaging outcomes are summarised in Table 2. The proportion of patients with a normal TSH at week 24 was 71% in the therapy group and 38% in the placebo group. Mean goitre volume changed by -18% with the therapy and -4% with placebo over the study period. Adverse events were similar between the two groups.

goldVerdicts.claimText: More patients achieved normal TSH with the therapy than with placebo
goldVerdicts.rationale: Support is a data table (71% vs 38%) with no narrative sentence stating it (rule 5).

goldVerdicts.claimText: The therapy significantly reduced goitre size
goldVerdicts.rationale: Ref reports an 18% vs 4% goitre reduction but states no significance; claim asserts 'significantly' (rule 1).
